ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana’s open interest dropping 30% in May, with price pressure near $80. Despite this, Solana’s on-chain activity and application revenue remain at record highs, signaling divergence between market sentiment and network usage.
ctxTalking:
• TrapDoor package attack targeting Solana, Sui, and Aptos wallet data through fake developer tools
• Solana futures open interest decline and speculation about further price drops
• Expansion of sports-related projects on Solana, including fantasy, prediction markets, and Chiliz Fan Tokens
ctxMatters:
Solana’s developer ecosystem faces new security threats from targeted malware campaigns.  
Market participants are watching for further price movement as futures interest wanes.  
Growth in sports applications highlights Solana’s appeal for large-scale consumer projects, even as token price fluctuates.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Sustained high application revenue and new sports industry integrations on Solana.
Noise: Short-term price speculation around $68 SOL without clear fundamental drivers.
ctxGlossary:
TrapDoor — A malware campaign distributing fake developer tools to steal sensitive crypto credentials  
Open Interest — The total value of outstanding futures contracts for an asset

readMore.title: Solana, Sui and Aptos wallet data targeted in TrapDoor package attack
Solana open interest drops 30% as altcoins slump
Building for the $400B+ Sports Industry on Solana
